text.paragraph:

Virginia Mason Issaquah 100 NE Gilman Blvd Issaquah, WA 98027- US (425) 557-8000

item.paragraph:
Soong MD, Derrick L: PERFORM
Event Display: PEDS Outpt Clinic Note
Authored Date: 20230320100222-0700

Assessment/Plan
_
1. CAP (community acquired pneumonia) J18.9
Significant cough throughout the exam, but he is in good spirits, and good energy.
Some signs of increased work of breathing, consistent with Pneumonia, but in good spirits with good energy.
Will start him on Amoxicillin x 7 days, will have him follow up on Wednesday, will also call to check in on him. Advised mom to start a dose as soon as she gets it and do another dose tonight.
Discussed worsening symptoms and going to the ER with Mom, she expressed understanding.
Orders:
amoxicillin 400 mg/5 mL oral liquid, 712 mg = 8.9 mL, PO, Q12 Hours, # 130 mL, x 7 day(s) 03/27/23
History of Present Illness
Started coughing back on March 10 and a Fever of 101F.
Sister got sick after him and got diagnosed with ear infection.
Mom states he has had a lot of fevers the last couple of days. Fever was pretty high up to 103F
Mom states he is lethargic and terrible and medication seems to turn him around.
Mom feels like she hears about of wheezing with the coughing.
He also complained of tummy pain on and off for the last couple of days.
No congestion or runny nose at this time.
He has some sore throat and lung pain from coughing.
They have tried some honey, which may help for a bit.
Review of Systems
Pertinent systems reviewed and negative except as per HPI
Physical Exam
Vitals & Measurements
T: 97.8 °F HR: 150(Pulse) RR: 54 SpO2: 92%
WT: 15.90 kg
CONSTITUTIONAL: Alert, interactive, and non-toxic in appearance.
EARS: Clear TMs
HENT: mucus membranes moist without lesions or exudates, no pharyngeal erythema
RESPIRATORY: Moving air well bilaterally, crackles heard in right lower lung base, some intercostal retractions with tracheal tugging, but able to speak in full sentences, no wheezing.
CARDIOVASCULAR: S1, S2, no murmurs or extra sounds
GASTROINTESTINAL: soft, nontender, nondistended
External Vitals & Measurements
No qualifying data available.
Growth Percentiles
Weight Percentile: 5.69 % (03/20/23 09:02:00)
Hearing and Vision Screenings
No qualifying data available.
Problem List/Past Medical History
Ongoing
Abdominal pain
Birth mark Comments: forehead
Chromosomal microduplication
Hemangioma
Prematurity
Strabismus
Underweight in childhood
Historical
No qualifying data
Medications
Albuterol (Eqv-ProAir HFA) 90 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol, 1 puff(s), ORAL INH, Q6 Hours, PRN
amoxicillin 400 mg/5 mL oral liquid, 712 mg= 8.9 mL, PO, Q12 Hours
Vitamin D, Daily
Allergies
NKA
Social History
Home/Environment
Lives with Father, Mother, Siblings, Grandmother.
Family History
Allergies: Mother and Father.
Anxiety: Mother.
Depression: Mother.
Diabetes mellitus type 2: Sister.
Eczema: Father and Pat. GFather.
Health Maintenance
Pending (in the next year)
There are no current recommendations pending
Due In Future
Ped - well child check not due until 12/19/23 and every 1 year(s)
Satisfied (in the past 1 year)
Satisfied
Ped - well child check on 12/19/22. Satisfied by Soong MD, Derrick L
(Electronically Signed on 03/20/23 10:02 AM)
Soong MD, Derrick L
